findings.title: Signal handler double-free confirmed with heap reuse, callback redirect blocked by NULL reset
findings.statement: The setuid daemon's SIGALRM handler frees the request buffer during the critical section between processing and release, creating a double-free in 40% of runs with a 50ms payload. The freed slot accepts a controlled replacement object in 85% of double-free cases. However, the signal handler also NULLs the file_write_cb pointer after the free, and the main loop checks for NULL before callback dispatch. Two alternative paths were evaluated and found non-viable.

evidence.summary: strace -e signal=SIGALRM: 8ms race window, SIGALRM during critical section in 40% of runs with 50ms payload
debug_gdb_commands: conditional breakpoint on free() confirms double-free of same pointer; heap-analysis shows controlled object at target slot
decomp_ghidra_decompile: signal handler NULLs file_write_cb after free; main loop NULL-checks before dispatch
